_comment: RPC error classification data used by src/rpc-error-classifier.js. Three buckets: transient (retry), terminalNonceUnused (abort, reset NonceManager because the rejected TX never consumed its nonce), terminalNonceConsumed (abort, leave NonceManager alone because the nonce is already used on-chain). Substrings are matched case-insensitively against the verbatim inner node error message; ethersCodes are matched against err.code exactly. Keep lists lowercase. JSON-RPC code -32000 alone is a catch-all and is NOT used for classification — the node's message substring is the reliable signal.

transient.description: Network/infra glitches where the TX may or may not have been broadcast. Safe to reset the NonceManager and retry.

terminalNonceUnused.description: Node rejected the TX before it was admitted to the executable pending pool. The nonce was never consumed on-chain so the NonceManager must be reset. Do NOT retry in place — the cause is typically persistent (saturation, bad params, insufficient funds).
messageSubstrings: max fee per gas less than block base fee

terminalNonceConsumed.description: The nonce is already used (either by this TX or a replacement). Abort and leave the NonceManager alone so the next TX uses nonce+1.
